Name: Butterfree V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Butterfree VMAX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Paras
Flavor-Text: The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.

Name: Parasect
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.

Name: Carnivine
Effects: You may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

Name: Pansage
Flavor-Text: It’s good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It’s kind enough to share them with friends.

Name: Simisage
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

Name: Karrablast
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its strange physiology reacts to electrical energy in interesting ways. The presence of a Shelmet will cause this Pokémon to evolve.

Name: Shelmet
Flavor-Text: When attacked, it tightly shuts the lid of its shell. This reaction fails to protect it from Karrablast, however, because they can still get into the shell.

Name: Accelgor
Flavor-Text: It moves with blinding speed and lobs poison at foes. Featuring Accelgor as a main character is a surefire way to make a movie or comic popular.

Name: Rowlet
Ability-Effect: If you played Bird Keeper from your hand during this turn, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon’s attack costs.
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: At a distance, it launches its sharp feathers while flying about. If the enemy gets too close, Rowlet switches tactics and delivers vicious kicks.

Name: Dartrix
Flavor-Text: It throws one knifelike feather after another at its enemies, and each one precisely strikes a weak point. These feathers are known as blade quills.

Name: Decidueye
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX.
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: As if wielding a bow, it launches the arrow quills hidden among the feathers of its wings. Decidueye’s shots never miss.

Name: Bounsweet
Effects: Search your deck for a [G] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its body gives off a sweet, fruity scent that is extremely appetizing to bird Pokémon.

Name: Steenee
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As it twirls like a dancer, a sweet smell spreads out around it. Anyone who inhales the scent will feel a surge of happiness.

Name: Tsareena
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
You may put an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon into their hand.
Flavor-Text: This feared Pokémon has long, slender legs and a cruel heart. It shows no mercy as it stomps on its opponents.

Name: Wimpod
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s nature’s cleaner—it eats anything and everything, including garbage and rotten things. The ground near its nest is always clean.

Name: Golisopod
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your opponent’s Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX in play.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It will do anything to win, taking advantage of every opening and finishing opponents off with the small claws on its front legs.

Name: Charizard V
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard VMAX
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Houndoom V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
If your Benched [R] Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 100 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Torchic
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Combusken
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: During a battle, the hot flame in its body increases. Its kicks have outstanding destructive power.

Name: Blaziken
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is [R] and [F] type.
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When facing a tough foe, it looses flames from its wrists. Its powerful legs let it jump clear over buildings.

Name: Heatran
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

Name: Pansear
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Simisear
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: When it gets excited, embers rise from its head and tail and it gets hot. For some reason, it loves sweets.

Name: Galarian Darmanitan
Effects: You may discard all [W] Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Anger has reignited its atrophied flame sac. This Pokémon spews fire everywhere as it rampages indiscriminately.

Name: Larvesta
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It protects itself with flame. Long years ago, people believed Larvesta had a nest on the sun.

Name: Volcarona
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has no damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: According to legends, it was hatched from a flaming cocoon to save people and Pokémon that were suffering from the cold.

Name: Fletchinder
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Its speed right after takeoff already puts it in the top speed class of all bird Pokémon.

Name: Talonflame
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Bird Pokémon make up most of its diet. It approaches at high speeds and smacks them down to the ground with its powerful kick.

Name: Centiskorch V
Effects: You may discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Centiskorch VMAX
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you did any damage with this attack, you may attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Mr. Mime
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its talent is tap-dancing. It can also manipulate temperatures to create a floor of ice, which this Pokémon can kick up to use as a barrier.

Name: Galarian Mr. Rime
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s face-down Prize cards with the top card of their deck. (The cards stay face down.)
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.
Flavor-Text: It’s highly skilled at tap-dancing. It waves its cane of ice in time with its graceful movements.

Name: Suicune
Effects: Put 2 [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Said to be the embodiment of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

Name: Feebas
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Although unattractive and unpopular, this Pokémon’s marvelous vitality has made it a subject of research.

Name: Milotic
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Milotic has provided inspiration to many artists. It has even been referred to as the most beautiful Pokémon of all.

Name: Relicanth
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Rare Fossil cards and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The reason it hasn’t changed at all in a hundred million years is that it’s apparently already a perfect life-form.

Name: Panpour
Flavor-Text: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

Name: Simipour
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It prefers places with clean water. When its tuft runs low, it replenishes it by siphoning up water with its tail.

Name: Galarian Darumaka
Flavor-Text: It lived in snowy areas for so long that its fire sac cooled off and atrophied. It now has an organ that generates cold instead.

Name: Galarian Darmanitan
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Crushing Headbutt.
Flavor-Text: On days when blizzards blow through, it comes down to where people live. It stashes food in the snowball on its head, taking it home for later.

Name: Vanillite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Unable to survive in hot areas, it makes itself comfortable by breathing out air cold enough to cause snow. It burrows into the snow to sleep.

Name: Vanillish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: By drinking pure water, it grows its icy body. This Pokémon can be hard to find on days with warm, sunny weather.

Name: Vanilluxe
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When its anger reaches a breaking point, this Pokémon unleashes a fierce blizzard that freezes every creature around it, be they friend or foe.

Name: Cubchoo
Flavor-Text: When this Pokémon is in good health, its snot becomes thicker and stickier. It will smear its snot on anyone it doesn’t like.

Name: Beartic
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It swims through frigid seas, searching for prey. From its frozen breath, it forms icy fangs that are harder than steel.

Name: Wishiwashi
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 8 cards.
Flavor-Text: Individually, they’re incredibly weak. It’s by gathering up into schools that they’re able to confront opponents.

Name: Mareanie
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The first symptom of its sting is numbness. The next is an itching sensation so intense that it’s impossible to resist the urge to claw at your skin.

Name: Toxapex
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon and heal all damage from it.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: To survive in the cold waters of Galar, this Pokémon forms a dome with its legs, enclosing its body so it can capture its own body heat.

Name: Dracovish
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can’t play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Powerful legs and jaws made it the apex predator of its time. Its own overhunting of its prey was what drove it to extinction.

Name: Arctovish
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 60 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Though it’s able to capture prey by freezing its surroundings, it has trouble eating the prey afterward because its mouth is on top of its head.

Name: Mareep
Flavor-Text: Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You’ll want to pet it because it’s cute, but if you use your bare hand, you’ll get a painful shock.

Name: Flaaffy
Flavor-Text: It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.

Name: Ampharos
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its tail shines bright and strong. It has been prized since long ago as a beacon for sailors.

Name: Electrike
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

Name: Manectric
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It stimulates its own muscles with electricity, so it can move quickly. It eases its soreness with electricity, too, so it can recover quickly as well.

Name: Vikavolt V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Item cards from their hand.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tapu Koko
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: The lightning-wielding guardian deity of Melemele, Tapu Koko is brimming with curiosity and appears before people from time to time.

Name: Toxel
Flavor-Text: It stores poison in an internal poison sac and secretes that poison through its skin. If you touch this Pokémon, a tingling sensation follows.

Name: Toxtricity
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 5 cards of your opponent’s deck. If tails, discard the top 5 cards of your deck.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Capable of generating 15,000 volts of electricity, this Pokémon looks down on all that would challenge it.

Name: Pincurchin
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

Name: Dracozolt
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Amping Up attack does 90 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: In ancient times, it was unbeatable thanks to its powerful lower body, but it went extinct anyway after it depleted all its plant-based food sources.

Name: Arctozolt
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The shaking of its freezing upper half is what generates its electricity. It has a hard time walking around.

Name: Jigglypuff
Flavor-Text: The songs they sing are totally different depending on the region they live in. Some even sound like they’re shouting!

Name: Wigglytuff
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When it gets angry, it inhales with all its might, and its body gradually inflates. Sometimes they can grow 20 times larger!

Name: Mew V
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Snubbull
Flavor-Text: In contrast to its appearance, it’s quite timid. When playing with other puppy Pokémon, it sometimes gets bullied.

Name: Granbull
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s popular with young people, Granbull is timid and sensitive, so it’s totally incompetent as a watchdog.

Name: Lunatone
Effects: Look at the top 4 cards of either player’s deck and put them back in any order.
This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It’s active on the night of a full moon.

Name: Gothita
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent’s deck and put them back in any order.
Flavor-Text: Though they’re still only babies, there’s psychic power stored in their ribbonlike feelers, and sometimes they use that power to fight.

Name: Gothorita
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that when stars shine in the night sky, this Pokémon will spirit away sleeping children. Some call it the Witch of Punishment.

Name: Gothitelle
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Choose 2 random cards from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It has tremendous psychic power, but it dislikes conflict. It’s also able to predict the future based on the movement of the stars.

Name: Golett
Flavor-Text: They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders.

Name: Golurk
Flavor-Text: Artillery platforms built into the walls of ancient castles served as perches from which Golurk could fire energy beams.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.
Flavor-Text: Its upper whiskers are sensors that survey its surroundings. Its lower whiskers are organs that shoot electricity.

Name: Morelull
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull’s head. The caps regrow overnight.

Name: Shiinotic
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack.
If Glimwood Tangle is in play, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

Name: Mimikyu
Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Benched Pokémon can’t be healed.
Flavor-Text: It wears a rag fashioned into a Pikachu costume in an effort to look less scary. Unfortunately, the costume only makes it creepier.

Name: Sinistea
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is said to have been born when a lonely spirit possessed a cold, leftover cup of tea.

Name: Polteageist
Ability-Effect: You must discard a Pokémon that has the Mad Party attack from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.
Flavor-Text: This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found.

Name: Diglett
Flavor-Text: If a Diglett digs through a field, it leaves the soil perfectly tilled and ideal for planting crops.

Name: Dugtrio
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A team of Diglett triplets. It triggers huge earthquakes by burrowing 60 miles underground.

Name: Larvitar
Flavor-Text: Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.

Name: Pupitar
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Even sealed in its shell, it can move freely. Hard and fast, it has outstanding destructive power.

Name: Tyranitar
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.
Discard the top 5 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its body can’t be harmed by any sort of attack, so it is very eager to make challenges against enemies.

Name: Trapinch
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its nest is a sloped, bowl-like pit in the desert. Once something has fallen in, there is no escape.

Name: Vibrava
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The ultrasonic waves it generates by rubbing its two wings together cause severe headaches.

Name: Flygon
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Active Pokémon can’t retreat.
Effects: If your opponent has a Stadium in play, discard it. If you discarded a Stadium in this way, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon hides in the heart of sandstorms it creates and seldom appears where people can see it.

Name: Solrock
Ability-Effect: If you have Lunatone in play, your opponent’s Pokémon in play have no Resistance.
Flavor-Text: When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.

Name: Hippopotas
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It moves through the sands with its mouth open, swallowing sand along with its prey. It gets rid of the sand by spouting it from its nose.

Name: Hippowdon
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Stones can get stuck in the ports on their bodies. Dwebble help dislodge such stones, so Hippowdon look after these Pokémon.

Name: Rhyperior V
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Heavy Rock Artillery.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Diggersby
Flavor-Text: With power equal to an excavator, it can dig through dense bedrock. It’s a huge help during tunnel construction.

Name: Passimian
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Displaying amazing teamwork, they follow the orders of their boss as they all help out in the search for their favorite berries.

Name: Galarian Sirfetch’d
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Meteor Assault again until it leaves the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: Only Farfetch’d that have survived many battles can attain this evolution. When this Pokémon’s leek withers, it will retire from combat.

Name: Galarian Slowbro V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Poisoned.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Grimer
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: The wastewater coming from factories is clean these days, so Grimer have nothing to eat. They’re said to be on the verge of extinction.

Name: Muk
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.
Flavor-Text: Because they scatter germs everywhere, they’ve long been targeted for extermination, leading to a steep decline in their population.

Name: Spinarak
Flavor-Text: Although the poison from its fangs isn’t that strong, it’s potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.

Name: Ariados
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Evolution Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.

Name: Crobat V
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. You can’t use more than 1 Dark Asset Ability each turn.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Darkrai
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached, it takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [D] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It steals things from people just to amuse itself with their frustration. A rivalry exists between this Pokémon and Nickit.

Name: Liepard
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Don’t be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

Name: Deino
Flavor-Text: When it encounters something, its first urge is usually to bite it. If it likes what it tastes, it will commit the associated scent to memory.

Name: Zweilous
Flavor-Text: While hunting for prey, Zweilous wanders its territory, its two heads often bickering over which way to go.

Name: Hydreigon
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a [D] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: There are a slew of stories about villages that were destroyed by Hydreigon. It bites anything that moves.

Name: Hoopa
Effects: If this Pokémon didn’t move from the Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does nothing. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness.
Flavor-Text: It gathers things it likes and passes them through its loop to teleport them to a secret place.

Name: Nickit
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

Name: Thievul
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

Name: Grimmsnarl V
Effects: Put 2 [D] Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Grimmsnarl VMAX
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each extra [D] Energy attached to this Pokémon (in addition to this attack’s cost). You can’t add more than 100 damage in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Eternatus V
Effects: You may attach a [D] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 120 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Eternatus VMAX
Ability-Effect: If all of your Pokémon in play are [D] type, you can have up to 8 Pokémon on your Bench, and you can’t put non-[D] Pokémon into play. (If this Ability stops working, discard Pokémon from your Bench until you have 5.)
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your [D] Pokémon in play.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Scizor V
Effects: Discard a Pokémon Tool and a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Scizor VMAX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its body is draped in steel armor. It looks heavy, but it can fly at speeds of up to 185 miles an hour!

Name: Aron
Flavor-Text: When it evolves, it sheds the steel carapace that covered its whole body and develops a new one.

Name: Lairon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It habitually shows off its strength with the sparks it creates by ramming its steel body into boulders.

Name: Aggron
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.
Flavor-Text: While seeking iron for food, it digs tunnels by breaking through bedrock with its steel horns.

Name: Escavalier
Effects: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 70 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 140 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They use shells they’ve stolen from Shelmet to arm and protect themselves. They’re very popular Pokémon in the Galar region.

Name: Klink
Effects: Search your deck for a [M] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The two minigears that compose this Pokémon are closer than twins. They mesh well only with each other.

Name: Klang
Effects: Search your deck for a [M] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: When Klang goes all out, the minigear links up perfectly with the outer part of the big gear, and this Pokémon’s rotation speed increases sharply.

Name: Klinklang
Effects: If you don’t have Klink and Klang on your Bench, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: From its spikes, it launches powerful blasts of electricity. Its red core contains an enormous amount of energy.

Name: Galarian Stunfisk V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon gets +20 HP for each [M] Energy attached to it.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 12 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Meltan
Flavor-Text: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

Name: Melmetal
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.

Name: Cufant
Flavor-Text: It digs up the ground with its trunk. It’s also very strong, being able to carry loads of over five tons without any problem at all.

Name: Copperajah
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be affected by any Special Conditions.
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They came over from another region long ago and worked together with humans. Their green skin is resistant to water.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: You shouldn’t get close to the child when it’s playing outside its mother’s pouch. Its mother is always nearby watching over it.

Name: Tauros
Flavor-Text: They live in groups. The one with the longest, thickest, and most-scarred horns is the boss of the herd.

Name: Sentret
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It stands on its tail so it can see a long way. If it spots an enemy, it cries loudly to warn its kind.

Name: Furret
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It makes a nest to suit its long and skinny body. The nest is impossible for other Pokémon to enter.

Name: Dunsparce
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: When it sees a person, it digs a hole with its tail to make its escape. If you happen to find one, consider yourself lucky.

Name: Teddiursa
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Before food becomes scarce in wintertime, its habit is to hoard food in many hidden locations.

Name: Ursaring
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Although it has a large body, it is quite skilled at climbing trees. It eats and sleeps in the treetops.

Name: Lugia
Effects: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

Name: Skitty
Effects: Search your deck for an Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It shows its cute side by chasing its own tail until it gets dizzy.

Name: Delcatty
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It is highly popular among female Trainers for its sublime fur. It does not keep a nest.

Name: Salamence V
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Salamence VMAX
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Max Wings.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Starly
Ability-Effect: If you played Bird Keeper from your hand during this turn, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon’s attack costs.
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

Name: Staravia
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in forests and fields. Squabbles over territory occur when flocks collide.

Name: Staraptor
Effects: Move any amount of Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

Name: Ducklett
Flavor-Text: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

Name: Swanna
Ability-Effect: If you played Bird Keeper from your hand during this turn, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon’s attack costs.
Effects: You may discard a card from your hand. If you do, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

Name: Bunnelby
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.
Flavor-Text: It excels at digging holes. Using its ears, it can dig a nest 33 feet deep in one night.

Name: Fletchling
Flavor-Text: Its body is always warm. Trainers who live in cold areas apparently sleep with it in their bed.

Name: Skwovet
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.

Name: Greedent
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn’t notice the loss.

Name: Rookidee
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokémon benefits from every battle—even a defeat increases its strength a bit.

Name: Corvisquire
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Smart enough to use tools in battle, these Pokémon have been seen picking up rocks and flinging them or using ropes to wrap up enemies.

Name: Corviknight
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 1 of your Pokémon, except any Corviknight, and all attached cards into your hand.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon reigns supreme in the skies of the Galar region. The black luster of its steel body could drive terror into the heart of any foe.

Name: Big Parasol
Description: As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon done to all of your Pokémon. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Billowing Smoke
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, that player discards any Prize cards they would take for that Knock Out instead of putting those cards into their hand.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Bird Keeper
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Cape of Toughness
Description: The Basic Pokémon this card is attached to gets +50 HP, except Pokémon-GX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Familiar Bell
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon with the same name as a Pokémon in your discard pile, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Glimwood Tangle
Description: Once during each player’s turn, after that player flips any coins for an attack, they may ignore all results of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Kabu
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If your Active Pokémon is your only Pokémon in play, draw 8 cards instead.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Old PC
Description: Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, put a card from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Piers
Description: Search your deck for an Energy card and a [D] Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Pokémon Breeder’s Nurturing
Description: Choose up to 2 of your Pokémon in play. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rare Fossil
Description: Play this card as if it were a 70-HP Basic [C] Pokémon. At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.This card can’t be affected by any Special Conditions, and it can’t retreat.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Rose
Description: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon VMAX. If you attached any Energy cards in this way, discard your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rose Tower
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may draw cards until they have 3 cards in their hand.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Spikemuth
Description: Whenever a player’s Active Pokémon moves to the Bench during their turn, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Struggle Gloves
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to has Weakness to your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s type, its attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Turbo Patch
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Basic Pokémon that isn’t a Pokémon-GX.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Yell Horn
Description: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Heat [R] Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [R] Energy.The [R] Pokémon this card is attached to gets +20 HP.

Name: Hiding [D] Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [D] Energy.The [D] Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost.

Name: Powerful [C] Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.The attacks of the [C] Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Butterfree V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Houndoom V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
If your Benched [R] Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 100 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Centiskorch V
Effects: You may discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Vikavolt V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Item cards from their hand.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rhyperior V
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Heavy Rock Artillery.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Crobat V
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. You can’t use more than 1 Dark Asset Ability each turn.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Scizor V
Effects: Discard a Pokémon Tool and a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Stunfisk V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon gets +20 HP for each [M] Energy attached to it.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 12 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Description: Choose up to 2 of your Pokémon in play. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rose
Description: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon VMAX. If you attached any Energy cards in this way, discard your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Butterfree VMAX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Centiskorch VMAX
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you did any damage with this attack, you may attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Eternatus VMAX
Ability-Effect: If all of your Pokémon in play are [D] type, you can have up to 8 Pokémon on your Bench, and you can’t put non-[D] Pokémon into play. (If this Ability stops working, discard Pokémon from your Bench until you have 5.)
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your [D] Pokémon in play.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Scizor VMAX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Salamence VMAX
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Max Wings.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Pokémon Breeder’s Nurturing
Description: Choose up to 2 of your Pokémon in play. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rose
Description: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon VMAX. If you attached any Energy cards in this way, discard your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rillaboom
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 [G] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

Name: Coalossal
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [R] Energy card, a [F] Energy card, or 1 of each from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It’s usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Big Parasol
Description: As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon done to all of your Pokémon. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Turbo Patch
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Basic Pokémon that isn’t a Pokémon-GX.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Capture Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.When you attach this card from your hand to a Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
